title: STANDARD FOR QUICK FROZEN FISH STICKS (FISH FINGERS), FISH PORTIONS AND FISH FILLETS - BREADED OR IN BATTER

sections.section: STANDARD FOR QUICK FROZEN FISH STICKS (FISH FINGERS), FISH PORTIONS AND FISH FILLETS - BREADED OR IN BATTER
sections.summary: Title of the Codex standard covering quick frozen fish sticks (fish fingers), fish portions and fish fillets, breaded or in batter.
Document code: CXS 166-1989.

sections.section: Adopted in 1989. Revised in 1995, 2004, 2017. Amended in 2011, 2013, 2014, 2016.
sections.summary: Adoption and amendment history: adopted 1989; revised 1995, 2004, 2017; amended 2011, 2013, 2014, 2016.

Applies to quick frozen breaded or battered fish sticks and portions (from blocks or formed) and to natural fish fillets, singly or combined, raw or partially cooked, offered for direct human consumption without further industrial processing.
Heading only — 'Description' section serving as parent for subsequent descriptive subsections.

Defines product types: a fish stick (including coating) must weigh 20–50 g, have length at least three times greatest width and be at least 10 mm thick; fish portions may be any shape/weight; sticks/portions may be from single or mixed species with similar sensory properties; fillets are slices removed parallel to the backbone, with or without skin.

Requires quick freezing to rapidly pass the range of maximum crystallization and be considered complete only when the product's thermal centre reaches −18°C or colder after thermal stabilization, and mandates keeping the product deep frozen during transport/storage; permits industrial repacking or further industrial processing under controlled conditions that maintain quality followed by re-application of the quick freezing process.
Allows any product presentation provided it meets all standard requirements and is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

Raw material requirements: fish must be fillets or minced flesh of edible species of a quality fit for sale fresh; coatings and all ingredients must be food-grade and conform to applicable Codex standards; frying fat/oil must be suitable for human consumption and the desired final product characteristics.
Specifies fish raw material: products shall be prepared from fish fillets, minced fish flesh or mixtures thereof from edible species of a quality such as to be sold fresh for human consumption.
States that the coating and all ingredients used therein must be of food grade quality and conform to all applicable Codex standards.
Requires any fat or oil used in cooking to be suitable for human consumption and appropriate for achieving the desired final product characteristics (see also Section 4).
Products must meet this Standard when lots examined per Section 9 comply with provisions of Section 8, and products shall be examined using the methods given in Section 7.
This is a section heading only (no substantive requirements or text provided for decomposition).

Permitted food additives for products conforming to this Standard include specified antioxidants, humectants, acidity regulators, thickeners, colours, emulsifiers, flavour enhancers and raising agents when used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for food category 09.2.2 and its parent categories.

Products should be prepared and handled in accordance with relevant Codex hygiene Codes (e.g., CXC 1-1969, CXC 52-2003, CXC 8-1976), must comply with any Codex microbiological criteria, be free from foreign material that poses a health threat, and when tested shall not contain microorganisms or microbial substances presenting a health hazard or other hazardous substances in amounts exceeding Codex standards; additionally, histamine must not exceed 20 mg/100 g for species in the listed families (Clupeidae, Scombridae, Scombresocidae, Pomatomidae and Coryphaenedae).

This is a labelling heading indicating that, in addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods, specific labelling provisions follow (no substantive text in this heading itself).

Labels must declare an appropriate name such as “breaded”/“battered”, “fish sticks”, “fish portions” or “fillets”, reference the species or mixture, declare the proportion of fish content, indicate “quick frozen” or “frozen” as applicable, state whether product is from minced fish flesh or fillets (or both), and state storage conditions to maintain quality during transport, storage and distribution.
The label must state that the product shall be stored at a temperature of -18 °C or colder.

Required information may appear on the container or accompanying documents, except the name of the food, lot identification, the name and address of manufacturer/packer, and storage instructions must always appear on the container, although lot identification and name/address may be replaced by an identifiable mark linked to accompanying documents.
This is a heading for sampling, examination and analysis (no substantive requirements in the heading itself).

Lot sampling for product examination must follow an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5 (sample unit: entire container for prepackaged goods; at least 1 kg for bulk-packed products), and sampling for net weight must follow a sampling plan meeting Codex criteria.
Net weight (exclusive of packaging) is determined on each whole primary container of each sample representing a lot and shall be determined in the frozen state.

Samples for sensory and physical examination shall be assessed by trained persons and in accordance with procedures in Sections 7.4–7.7, Annex A and CXG 31-1999 (Guidelines for Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories).

Estimation of fish content uses AOAC Method 996.15 (End Product Method) with % Fish Content = (Wd/Wb) × 100 + Adjustment Factor, where Wd is debattered/debreaded weight and Wb is battered/breaded weight, and adjustment factors are 2.0% for raw breaded and batter-dipped products and 4.0% for precooked products (ref. J. AOAC Int. 80, 1235 (1997)).

Estimation of fish content uses AOAC Method 996.15 (End Product Method) with % Fish Content = (Wd/Wb) × 100 + Adjustment Factor, where Wd is debattered/debreaded weight and Wb is battered/breaded weight, and adjustment factors are 2.0% for raw breaded and batter-dipped products and 4.0% for precooked products (ref. J. AOAC Int. 80, 1235 (1997)).
Determination of gelatinous conditions is to be performed according to specified AOAC moisture methods for meat and meat products (e.g., AOAC 983.18 and AOAC 950.46).

sections.section: 7.6 Estimation of Proportion of Fish Fillets and Minced Fish Flesh
sections.summary: Estimation of the proportion of fish fillets and minced fish flesh is addressed in Annex B (see Annex B for procedure).

Frozen samples must be cooked prior to sensory assessment according to package instructions or, if none available, using AOAC procedure 976.16 (heat to internal 65–70 °C), with cooking time varying by product size and equipment.

Histamine determination methods must meet specified performance criteria (including minimum applicable range, LOD, LOQ, RSDR and recovery) for MLs of 10 mg/100 g (average) and 20 mg/100 g (each unit), and applicable methods cited include AOAC 977.13 and NMKL methods.
This is a heading introducing definitions of defectives, stating the sample unit is defective when it exhibits any of the properties defined below (no substantive definitions in the heading itself).

For packs designated boneless, a cooked sample is defective if it contains more than one bone per kg that is ≥10 mm in length or ≥1 mm in diameter; a bone ≤5 mm in length is not a defect if its diameter ≤2 mm, and the foot of a bone is disregarded if its width ≤2 mm or it can be easily stripped off with a fingernail.
Defines as a defect (cooked state) any sample unit with persistent, distinct objectionable odour or flavour indicative of decomposition, rancidity or feed.
Specifies flesh-abnormality defects: gelatinous fish core with >86% moisture in any individual fillet, or pasty texture from parasites affecting >5% of the sample unit by weight.

Sets lot acceptance criteria: total defectives per Section 8 must not exceed the sampling-plan acceptance number (c) at AQL 6.5; average fish flesh ≥50% of frozen weight; average net weight ≥ declared weight with no unreasonable shortage in any container; and compliance with Food Additives, Hygiene and Labelling requirements of Sections 4–6.

sections.section: ESTIMATION OF PROPORTION OF FISH FILLETS AND MINCED FISH FLESH
sections.summary: Describes the WEFTA method to estimate proportion of fillets and minced flesh, listing equipment (balance sensitive to 0.1 g; 200 mm circular sieve with 2.5/2.8 mm openings, rubber-edge spatula, forks, plates, water‑tight bags), sampling to ~200 g subunits, weighing frozen samples (weight A), thawing in an agitated water bath ≈20°C (≤35°C), draining thaw drip for 2 minutes on a sieve inclined 17–20°, determining drained sample weight (B) and thaw drip (A−B), and separating minced flesh from fillet using fork and spatula.
